title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week focused on making OpsPal more reliable and self-maintaining. The platform now automatically detects and fixes common setup issues, including missing dependencies and outdated configurations. Most importantly, OpsPal added better safety checks for Salesforce metadata changes, preventing errors when Dynamic Forms are active and ensuring new record types work properly.

entries.headline: HubSpot connector now has full feature parity with Salesforce
OpsPal automatically cleans up temporary files and old session data
details: Removes outdated cache files older than 7 days
Clears abandoned session locks older than 5 minutes
Alerts you when memory usage files get too large

entries.headline: OpsPal removes outdated connectors automatically across all systems
details: Works on Windows, Mac, and Linux systems
Detects and removes any inactive marketplace connectors
Cleans up all plugin types, not just specific ones

entries.headline: Unused connectors are removed every time you start OpsPal
Salesforce metadata changes now work properly with Dynamic Forms
details: Prevents invisible field additions when Dynamic Forms are active
New record types automatically get proper permissions
Routing conflicts no longer block your sessions

entries.headline: OpsPal automatically installs missing dependencies when you start
details: Shows clear error messages when installations fail
Provides manual fix commands when needed

entries.headline: Eliminated false warnings about missing packages at startup
